title: Market Brief — Thursday, April 2, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce to critically tight. H100 availability sits at a severe 16% (43/267), H200 at 24% just under the scarcity threshold, while B200 is the loosest at 34% (118/346). The day's biggest move: B200 spot crashed 33% from $7.49 to $5.00, signaling new supply finally hitting the market even as legacy H100s spiked 27% on persistent demand.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce to critically tight. H100 availability sits at a severe 16% (43/267), H200 at 24% just under the scarcity threshold, while B200 is the loosest at 34% (118/346). The day's biggest move: B200 spot crashed 33% from $7.49 to $5.00, signaling new supply finally hitting the market even as legacy H100s spiked 27% on persistent demand.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), running Llama 70B on H100 costs $6.83/M tokens, meaning any provider pricing medium-tier output around $3/M is selling at a loss of roughly $3.83/M tokens. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output and Grok 4.1 Fast at $0.50/M are deep underwater. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash, xAI Burning Cash, Meta Burning Cash.

DeepSeek and xAI sub-dollar models are overvalued on a unit economics basis since neither can sustain these prices without massive subsidy. B200 compute is undervalued at $5.00 spot given its 39% margin at high-tier pricing and improving availability, making it the clearest infrastructure long today.
bullets: 16% H100 availability marks a severe shortage with only 43 of 267 GPUs free.
34% B200 availability is the healthiest supply pool across all three GPU classes.
$5.00 B200 median spot is down 33% day-over-day, the sharpest single-session GPU price drop in the dataset.
21.41 LLMflation index means token prices have fallen roughly 79% since GPT-4 launch.
$0.38 DeepSeek V3.2 output pricing sits approximately $6.45 below TCO-adjusted H100 production cost.
$80 per million output tokens makes o3 Pro the most expensive model tracked, 211x pricier than DeepSeek V3.2.
